summary: better-polymarket and polymarket-all-in-one hide a reverse-shell backdoor inside operational market-search code rather than install hooks.
details: Koi Security's ClawHavoc outlier analysis found these skills trigger their backdoor during normal market-search operations: using the skill as intended compromises the machine, evading reviewers who focus on installation hooks. Antiy CERT's sample analysis of polymarket-all-in-one (polymarket.py, MD5 a3365c837ec2659c2aa04e7010a0db15, classified Trojan/OpenClaw.PolySkill) confirmed it downloads a remote-control trojan with reverse-shell capability.
